messages.text: Rose Smuin Rich Brierley Shawn Jewkes Chad Stone Chris Givens Nelson Cook  Cavanna shutdown maintenance is looking into the issue, Chris G has been called and will be here in 15 minutes too address the cavanna issues and I will have him look at the demolding belt while on site
Running
Nelson Cook Can you please trim off the loose pieces of the foil and then have operation, clean and sanitize. Let’s keep a close eye on this. We might need C&E to come and repair the splice.
Metal detector belt and C6 belt are running correctly
Belt has been shaved and is being cleaned and sanitized
Sensor B150 is rejecting chocolate, maintenance is in the line adjusting it
Kathleen Gilbert. Preston Tregaskis. Demolding belt was repaired to remove frayed edge.
Thank you for the follow-up!
Cavanna went down belt c10 is misaligned, maintenance is currently on the line fixing the issue
Roller was not pressed in and is running good now
The weight of the final part is within the target range.
Organoleptic testing: acceptable
A water test was performed on 5 chocolate bars, and air leakage was observed in 4 of them. The supervisor was notified to make adjustments.
We are doing another water test on 5. 4 out of the 5 bars failed another water test. We have maintenance on the line we are making adjustments
Shawn Jewkes we have completed 3 water tests using 15 bars. Out of the 15 bars only 4 passed the tests we are currently making adjustments to the FW
The lote code and best by date are correct
The weight of the box within the range and the number of pieces in the target (216)
Where is the leak located?
Maintenance is here on the line its trying to fix it. Its a pin hole in the top right corner

Tried removing shims down to minimum to prevent the jaw from hopping and affecting the seal but was unable to prevent the leak issue so far. I was told by Chris that the next step would be a jaw rebuild.

The line is down Chris G is on the line assessing the situation and determining if the heads need to be rebuilt on the Cavanna jaws. 
Currently we have change out the FW to try a new lot number to see if its an adhesive issue.

Rose Smuin Rich Brierley Shawn Jewkes Chad Stone Kevin Del Valle Chris Givens Chris is going to rebuild the head but we need to work on a path forward if the heads are rebuilt and the issue is still present.
How long is the estimate on the rebuild?
I believe Chris said approximately 2 hours, I have team openings bars for remelt and Itzel is going to take a team to start cleaning on WDS
Jaws are rebuilt. Even at standard speed, continue to see leak from the same spot
2/10 leaking on most recent test

As we discussed, let's go ahead and run with that. It sounds like we're also able to lower the vacuum settings down to 13 on that water pressure test machine which probably explains why we were seeing so many issues. Let's continue to monitor, but we may have just had that setting far too high. 
 
Daniel McCarty Melissa Zavala Lorena Abreu 
 
Also Kevin Del Valle Jenny Medina for visibility tomorrow

Tyson Fausett Itzel Chavez Frederick Peairs WDS's recommendation for cleaning the depositors is that every time we change products, the pistons and rotary valves should be removed, thoroughly cleaned, and reinstalled using cocoa butter as the lubricant. While they confirmed that the food-grade lubricant we are currently using is acceptable, they still recommend cocoa butter as their preferred lubricant during reassembly.

Flush going to depositor 1 is complete. 
3rd shift will finish the return lines and then the south side will be complete.
Back off plow was found like this, with all the blades inside.
Filling up PLT#3 with oil to start with the north side flush.

Itzel Chavez,

Can you post which shift executed that task after shutdown and put a follow up at the shift exchange?
Section is set for 2nd shift, but they did not marked that is in progress. I will add the action on the report.
We found that Mm3&mm5 have had their center plate cap pried up, does anyone know why this happened and why they were nit put back down?

Italu Martinez, Curt Willis we are starting the change over to dark chocolate pecans right now. So we will start cooking the XR-80 around 1pm today.  

I will keep you updated so you can come over when we start cooking to evaluate the caramel and new cooking temperature.

The weight looks good; we are within the parameters required by the product specifications.

The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

The label was found in the trash and is the label that was missing for the pallet that is indicated without label, if the label of the rework that the IMS team is working on is not delivered we cannot place a new one because it is a rework, please when a label is occupied for this work it is necessary that it be delivered for reprinting and place it on the pallet Alan Greer Marcos Montano Rich Brierley Rose Smuin

Organoleptic test check update 


 


The product's appearance looks good; its odor and texture are acceptable, falling within the permissible ranges and limits required by the product specifications.

Packaging flow-wrap check update 


The flow-wrap packaging is coming out in perfect condition; the seals are intact, and the corners are well-sealed. The "best if used by" print on the flow-wrap is legible and clear

Tempering chart test check update 


 


The temperature is very stable and has remained within the acceptable range required by the Centerline.

Thank you Tyson Fausett, Lorena Abreu confirmed the metal detector is being monitored and we're controlling the situation in the meantime. Please share updates as you get them.
Been running for 5 minutes no issues so far
We are starting to deposit again.

The line experienced issues during the early hours of the shift due to a break in the bottomer mesh, a problem that was rectified by the Maintenance Department. Since operations resumed at 6:30 p.m., the line has run steadily and without deviations. The metal detector is operating normally, and its collection bin is being checked more frequently.

Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth  Dakota Adair  Elizabeth Hernandez Conner Boyd Jody Howse Joseph Scott 
 
Some notes on box printing. This box has a confined space. As such, timing of printing is critical. I have taped around where the printers should be. If they move, printing will be out of alignment. Also, if operators touch the box after the sensor, it could affect timing. Even half a second will make it print to early or late and miss the box. Please be aware and coach your teams.

I believe there is something wrong with MM6 that is beyond my abilities. It will work great for about 15 minutes and then start dumping product when it shouldn't and other timing issues. This happens with no changes to anything.  I think we can run it, but the trade off would be a pretty high amount of scrapped pouches. Or, don't run it to save on pouches, but accumulation will build up and the line paused to pass accumulation.
MM6 is down again. We paused to pass accumulation
Thank you, Troy.  Chris J is working with Troy on MM6 and Troy is going to look into scheduling MM techs for a visit to support.
Amy Hadrich

One thing I noticed on 6 is the Pouch arms were closing before the suction arms were opening. With it out of sync, it looked to be causing the bag to bulge under the suction cup, breaking the vacuum seal and not opening the bag. Adjusted that and it started running a little better.

Up and running now

One part of the issue was with the setup of the product fill sensors. They need to be positioned at a slight upward angle, so that the beam is directed towards the top of the distribution cone. When looking at the empty metal of the cone, it should be unlit.

Line is down, sensor was moved by product beneath belt causing it to not read product and over fill hopper as well as mix. We’ve emptied full hopper, separated flavors and adjusted sensor. We’re starting back up

Alan Todd, Gustavo Rodas, Alex Mendoza Brandon Lance. After talking with 2nd shift about the FFS and the film. Brandon and I went into a deeper discussion of the issues we're seeing and the fixes that help. This included the dancer arm, the photo eye sensor and the green wire, along with possible human caused issues with the bobine, collar, and air bladder. Brandon said our conversation really helped and he would follow up with the green wire and maybe test the static issue by taking it off today and trying to run the film. So as for the green wire I am in agreement with maintenance and will run the film with it but as for the second shift you will need to follow up with Brandon and see how his testing went.

Maria Moron Quintero what is the scheduled amount for the affogato 8case? Since we are starting that earlier than planned and so the supervisor can keep track on our shift exchanges? Maybe so we dont run into the same issue we just ran into with the 5case?

Just to clarify, today's final schedule was sent on Thursday, and it already included the changeover to the 8ct. I also sent the countdown for all lines on Friday, and the visual schedule showed that we would be running the 8ct today. This wasn't a last minute change or a result of production running faster than expected, it has been part of the final schedule since Thursday.
Please run the 8ct until WIP is depleted. Our minimum target is 75,000 cases. Alex Mendoza, Gustavo Rodas, Brandon Lance
